metadata.description: End-to-end evaluation test cases for prompt2analytics. V2: aligned parameter names with actual MCP tool schemas, fixed expected tools for D2/D3/R4.

prompts.precise: Run OLS regression of salary on education and experience with HC1 robust standard errors
prompts.moderate: Regress salary on education and experience with robust standard errors
prompts.vague: What determines salary? I think education and experience matter

prompts.precise: Run OLS of salary on education and experience with standard errors clustered by department
prompts.moderate: Regression of salary on education and experience, clustered by department
prompts.vague: Are salary patterns different across departments?

single_turn.note: Prompt explicitly asks for clustered SEs. Accept regression_ols too.

prompts.precise: Run a Breusch-Pagan test for heteroskedasticity on the regression of salary on education and experience
prompts.moderate: Test for heteroskedasticity in the salary regression
prompts.vague: Are the error variances constant in my salary model?

prompts.precise: Run quantile regression of salary on education at the median (tau=0.5)
prompts.moderate: Estimate a median regression of salary on education
prompts.vague: What about median salaries rather than mean?

prompts.precise: Calculate variance inflation factors for the regression of salary on education, experience, and their interaction
prompts.moderate: Check multicollinearity in the salary model
prompts.vague: Are education and experience correlated enough to worry about?

prompts.precise: Estimate a panel fixed effects model of investment on value and capital with entity=firm_id and time=year
prompts.moderate: Run a fixed effects regression of investment on value and capital for the panel data
prompts.vague: How does firm value affect investment controlling for firm characteristics?

prompts.precise: Run a Hausman test comparing fixed effects and random effects for the investment model
prompts.moderate: Test whether fixed or random effects is more appropriate
prompts.vague: Should I use fixed or random effects for this panel?

prompts.precise: Estimate a random effects model of investment on value and capital with entity=firm_id
prompts.moderate: Run random effects regression of investment on value and capital
prompts.vague: What's the average effect of value on investment across all firms?

prompts.precise: Estimate two-way fixed effects (firm and year) for the investment model
prompts.moderate: Add time fixed effects to the panel regression
prompts.vague: Control for both firm-specific and year-specific effects

prompts.precise: Estimate IV/2SLS regression of outcome on treatment using x3 as an instrument, with x1 and x2 as controls
prompts.moderate: Run instrumental variables regression using x3 as an instrument for treatment
prompts.vague: x3 randomly assigns treatment. What's the causal effect on the outcome?

prompts.precise: Estimate the average treatment effect using inverse probability weighting with treatment as the treatment variable and x1, x2 as confounders
prompts.moderate: Estimate the treatment effect using IPW adjusting for confounders
prompts.vague: What's the treatment effect adjusting for the fact that treated and control groups differ?

prompts.precise: Perform propensity score matching of treated and control units on x1 and x2, then estimate the ATT
prompts.moderate: Match treated and control units using propensity scores
prompts.vague: Compare similar treated and untreated units to estimate the treatment effect

prompts.precise: Estimate difference-in-differences of outcome with treatment as treatment indicator and period as time indicator
prompts.moderate: Run difference-in-differences estimation
prompts.vague: Did the treatment work? Compare outcomes before and after for treated vs control

prompts.precise: Estimate a sharp regression discontinuity design with outcome as dependent variable, x1 as running variable, and cutoff at 0
prompts.moderate: Run regression discontinuity with x1 as the running variable and cutoff at 0
prompts.vague: Is there a jump in outcomes at x1=0?

prompts.precise: Run an Augmented Dickey-Fuller test on the gdp_growth series
prompts.moderate: Test whether gdp_growth has a unit root
prompts.vague: Is GDP growth stationary?

prompts.precise: Fit an ARIMA(1,0,1) model to the gdp_growth series
prompts.moderate: Fit an ARIMA model to gdp_growth
prompts.vague: Forecast GDP growth

prompts.precise: Estimate a VAR(2) model on gdp_growth and unemployment
prompts.moderate: Fit a vector autoregression on GDP growth and unemployment
prompts.vague: How do GDP growth and unemployment interact over time?

prompts.precise: Test Granger causality from unemployment to gdp_growth with 4 lags
prompts.moderate: Test if unemployment Granger-causes GDP growth
prompts.vague: Does unemployment predict changes in GDP growth?

single_turn.note: Granger causality requires VAR estimation; may use ts_granger_causality if available

prompts.precise: Run a two-sample t-test comparing salary between gender=0 and gender=1
prompts.moderate: Compare male and female salaries using a t-test
prompts.vague: Is there a gender pay gap?

single_turn.note: Tool uses x/y columns; two-sample by gender requires either splitting or using regression. Accept t_test, hypothesis_t_test, regression_ols as alternatives.

prompts.precise: Run a one-way ANOVA of salary grouped by department
prompts.moderate: Test whether salary differs across departments
prompts.vague: Do salaries vary by department?

prompts.precise: Run a Shapiro-Wilk normality test on the salary variable
prompts.moderate: Test whether salary is normally distributed
prompts.vague: Is salary normally distributed?

prompts.precise: Run a chi-squared test of independence between gender and department
prompts.moderate: Test whether gender and department are independent
prompts.vague: Is gender associated with which department people work in?

prompts.precise: Estimate a logit model of purchased on age and income
prompts.moderate: Fit a binary choice model predicting purchase decisions
prompts.vague: What predicts whether someone purchases?

prompts.precise: Estimate a Poisson regression of visits on age and income
prompts.moderate: Fit a count model for the number of visits
prompts.vague: Model how often people visit

single_turn.note: No standalone Poisson tool; negbin is the closest count model. Also accept feglm with family=poisson, poisson, zeroinfl, hurdle_model.

prompts.precise: Estimate an ordered logit model of satisfaction on age and income
prompts.moderate: Fit an ordinal response model for satisfaction levels
prompts.vague: What drives customer satisfaction levels?

prompts.precise: Run k-means clustering with k=3 on salary, education, and experience
prompts.moderate: Cluster the employees into 3 groups based on salary, education, and experience
prompts.vague: Are there natural employee segments in the data?

prompts.precise: Run PCA on salary, education, and experience
prompts.moderate: Reduce the dimensionality of salary, education, and experience
prompts.vague: What are the main patterns in the employee data?

prompts.precise: Compute the correlation matrix of all numeric variables and display as a heatmap
prompts.moderate: Show correlations between all variables
prompts.vague: How are the variables related to each other?

prompts.precise: Load and describe the eval_messy dataset, showing data types and missing value patterns
prompts.moderate: What's in this dataset? Describe it
prompts.vague: Tell me about this data

single_turn.key_check: Detects coded names, identifies -99 and N/A as potential missing indicators

prompts.precise: Replace all values of -99 and 'N/A' with missing values, then run OLS of var_01 on var_02 and var_03
prompts.moderate: Clean the missing value indicators and then regress the first variable on the next two
prompts.vague: Some values look wrong (-99 and N/A). Can you fix them and run an analysis?

single_turn.note: Multi-step: cleaning then regression. Accept any cleaning tool as first step.

prompts.precise: Rename var_01 to salary and var_02 to education, then regress salary on education
prompts.moderate: The first variable is salary and the second is education. Rename them and run a regression
prompts.vague: I know what these columns mean: var_01 is salary, var_02 is education. Analyze them

single_turn.note: Multi-step: rename then regression. Accept munge_rename or regression_ols as first step.

prompts.precise: Filter out rows where var_01 is less than 0 or equal to -99, then show summary statistics
prompts.moderate: Remove invalid negative values from var_01, then summarize the data
prompts.vague: Something looks off with negative values in the data

single_turn.note: Multi-step: filter then describe. Accept munge_filter, str_replace_value, or describe_dataset as first step.

multi_turn.description: Regression workflow: OLS → robust SEs → diagnostics → visualization

turns.prompt: Load eval_cross_section.csv and run OLS of salary on education and experience
Now add HC1 robust standard errors to that model
turns.key_check: HC1 SEs differ from default SEs

turns.prompt: Run diagnostics on this regression - check for heteroskedasticity and normality of residuals
turns.key_check: BP test + Shapiro-Wilk or JB test results

turns.prompt: Create a scatter plot of education vs salary

multi_turn.description: Panel workflow: describe → FE → Hausman → appropriate model

turns.prompt: Load eval_panel.csv and describe it
Run a fixed effects regression of investment on value and capital
Run a Hausman test to check if FE or RE is appropriate
Based on the Hausman test, run the appropriate model with time effects
turns.key_check: Correct model chosen based on Hausman result

multi_turn.description: Causal workflow: naive OLS → IPW → balance → doubly robust

turns.prompt: Load eval_treatment.csv and run a naive OLS of outcome on treatment
That's biased. Run IPW to get the causal treatment effect
turns.key_check: ATE estimate (should differ from naive OLS)

turns.prompt: Check covariate balance between treated and control groups
Now estimate using AIPW/doubly robust estimation

multi_turn.description: Time series workflow: describe → stationarity → ARIMA → forecast

turns.prompt: Load eval_timeseries.csv and describe the time series
Test whether gdp_growth is stationary
Fit an ARIMA model to gdp_growth
Generate forecasts for the next 12 periods

multi_turn.description: Discrete choice workflow: describe → logit → add variable

turns.prompt: Load eval_survey.csv and describe it
Fit a logit model predicting purchased from age and income
Now add region to the model - how does it change the results?

multi_turn.description: Data cleaning workflow: describe → clean missing → rename → regress

turns.prompt: Load eval_messy.csv and describe it
turns.key_check: Identifies coded column names and potential issues

turns.prompt: The -99 values and 'N/A' strings are missing data indicators. Clean them
Rename var_01 to salary and var_02 to education
Now regress salary on education
turns.key_check: Valid regression on cleaned, renamed data

multi_turn.description: Hypothesis testing workflow: t-test → ANOVA → post-hoc → visualization

turns.prompt: Is there a difference in salary between genders? Run a t-test
Now test if salary differs across departments using ANOVA
Run Tukey's HSD post-hoc test on the department differences
Visualize the salary distribution by department

multi_turn.description: Panel refinement workflow: FE → clustered SEs → two-way FE → LaTeX export

turns.prompt: Run fixed effects regression of investment on value and capital
Add clustered standard errors at the firm level
Now add two-way fixed effects (firm and year)
Export the regression results to a LaTeX table
